categories.title: Source Intake & Content Inventory
categories.summary: The repo needs a clear inventory of what comes from the staged `input/` snapshot and what comes from separate family-archive sources.

categories.title: Canonical Content Model & Transformation
categories.summary: The repo should turn imported source material into canonical site data rather than binding directly to the first presentation format it receives.

categories.title: Information Architecture & Navigation
categories.summary: The site should make the book easy to traverse by chapter, topic, and companion material without losing narrative continuity.

categories.title: Companion Media & Archive Materials
categories.summary: The website should package scans, audio, and reunion extras as first-class materials without obscuring what belongs to the core book.

categories.title: Site Experience & Presentation
categories.summary: This repo eventually needs a real website runtime and design system, but it does not have one yet.

categories.title: Provenance, Editorial Controls & Publishing
categories.summary: The repo should make imports, changes, and publication decisions inspectable.

categories.title: Accessibility, Performance & Delivery
categories.summary: The final site should be readable, fast enough, and robust across devices and connection quality.

categories.title: AI Harnesses & Content Ops
categories.summary: AI should reduce repetitive cleanup and comparison work, but the outputs still need inspectable records and human review where the archive demands it.

categories.title: Planning Infrastructure
categories.summary: The repo needs enough planning structure to survive long gaps and handoffs without turning into process theater.

coverage_matrix.notes: Canonical machine-readable inventory of upstream content surfaces and companion archive inputs.

sequencing_bias.reason: Use the current whole-book reading surface as the working website, refine it manually toward ship-readiness, and add deterministic tooling only when it removes repeated grunt work, protects against content loss, or clearly unlocks the next visible site improvement.

campaigns.notes: The intake seam and first real whole-book shell now exist. The current campaign should refine that real site toward shipping quality, using manual website shaping and UI scouting first, then adding thin helpers where repetition justifies them.

sections.title: Recommended Order
sections.markdown: Current priority: refine the existing whole-book reading surface into a site that feels ready to ship, using UI scouting and manual editorial cleanup on real pages. Add deterministic tooling or deeper structure only when it removes repeated grunt work, protects against content loss, or makes the visible site better.

ui_scout.pass_rule: A WB1 pass requires explicit desktop and mobile proof; mobile is not a spot-check.

upstream_integration.label: Upstream Integration
upstream_integration.notes: Primary risk surface while the repo decides how it consumes the staged source material in `input/`.

content_model.label: Content Model
content_model.notes: The local builder now normalizes manifest, article, image, and provenance inputs enough to render the full staged book and emit an omission audit, but broader canonical records still need to stabilize before wide UI work.

site_experience.label: Site Experience
site_experience.notes: A thin local whole-book reading surface now exists with grouped navigation and explicit omission accounting. This is the working website for the current phase, so manual refinement and repeated UI scouting should shape it before another runtime or data layer is invented.

methodology_tooling.label: Methodology Tooling
methodology_tooling.notes: Keep the imported methodology package honest and lightweight for this repo.

surfaces.label: Onward book core HTML
surfaces.upstream: local staged website snapshot in input/
surfaces.target_surface: whole-book local reading surface plus future canonical chapter and section records
surfaces.notes: Primary authored book body for the website, staged locally rather than pulled from a live repo integration. The current local builder now renders every manifest entry into a whole-book reading surface and emits an omission audit, while broader canonical records and companion media remain future work.

surfaces.label: Chapter audiobook links
surfaces.upstream: repo-owned audiobook manifest and MP3 files under audiobook/
surfaces.notes: The local whole-book builder now maps reviewed MP3 tracks from `audiobook/manifest.json` onto matching chapter and supplement pages, but richer companion metadata and off-site distribution lanes still remain future work.

surfaces.label: Full book audio
surfaces.upstream: repo-owned audiobook manifest and MP3 files under audiobook/
surfaces.notes: The local builder now emits a dedicated `audiobook.html` page, publishes a merged full-audiobook MP3 when built from `audiobook/manifest.json`, and exposes direct browser play/download controls, but the off-site distribution lane is still unresolved.

surfaces.label: Chapter podcast episodes
surfaces.notes: Podcast material connected to individual chapters.

surfaces.label: Full book podcast
surfaces.notes: Long-form podcast coverage for the whole book.

surfaces.label: Scanned companion materials
surfaces.notes: The first repo-owned scanned supplement now ships as the Rolland Alain memoir bundle plus preserved PDF source, but broader supplement galleries and additional family-archive scans still remain future work.
